target_phenotype: Morphological cell phenotype — cell and nuclear shape, texture, and intensity features across 2 imaging channels (DNA, centromere)

readout.measurement: High-content fluorescence imaging of fixed cells
readout.instrument_or_platform: Optical pooled screening (in situ sequencing + imaging)
readout.primary_metric: Morphological feature vectors — genes are clustered by similarity of phenotypic profiles across imaging channels

clustering.method: Leiden clustering on PHATE-embedded phenotypic profiles

notes: Interphase-gated cells only. 2-channel screen focused on DNA and centromere morphology (CENPA). At resolution k=15, this produces a high number of clusters (414), many of which may be small or noisy. Clusters represent sets of genes whose knockout produces similar morphological phenotypes.
